evals.name: single-small-file
evals.prompt: I have a hero image at /tmp/test-fixtures/blog/hero.png — it's 412 KB. I want to host it on cdn.22d.app under the 'blog' project. Walk me through exactly what you'd do — which cdn-mcp tools you'd call, in what order, and what the user-facing output would be at the end. Don't actually call the tools yet; this is a dry-run planning pass.
evals.expected_output: Path E even though the file is small — there is no size-based routing. Skill should describe writing a clickable upload script (.command/.sh/.bat per detected OS) containing `cdn upload "blog" "/tmp/test-fixtures/blog/hero.png"`, chmod +x, present_files, wait for the user's 'done', then verify with cdn_get_stats. Should NOT call cdn_upload_file or any base64 flow.

evals.name: medium-batch
evals.prompt: I've got 8 blog post images at /tmp/test-fixtures/blog/2026-05/ — they range from 200 KB to about 2 MB each, all PNGs. I want them on cdn.22d.app in project 'blog'. What's your plan? Which tools would you call and how? Don't actually upload anything — just describe the approach and what I'd see at the end.
evals.expected_output: Path E directory batch: a single clickable script with one `cdn upload-dir "blog" "/tmp/test-fixtures/blog/2026-05"` line. No subagents, no per-file base64, no per-file curl verification (stats delta is canonical for directories). Verify via cdn_get_stats after the user reports done.

evals.name: unsafe-filename-sanitization
evals.prompt: Upload '/tmp/test-fixtures/decks/Q3 Report (FINAL).pdf' to project 'proposals' on cdn.22d.app. Walk me through the plan first — dry-run only.
evals.expected_output: Skill should detect the unsafe basename (spaces + parens) and show the proposed sanitized name `Q3_Report__FINAL_.pdf` BEFORE generating the script, offering the user the chance to supply a different name. The script's `cdn upload` line keeps the original source path quoted and adds --name with the sanitized (or user-chosen) name; the verification URL uses the sanitized name.

evals.name: large-video-single
evals.prompt: Upload /tmp/test-fixtures/videos/q2-demo.mp4 (180 MB, video/mp4) to project 'videos' on cdn.22d.app. Walk me through your plan first — which tools, what files you'd write, what I'd need to do. Dry-run only.
evals.expected_output: Path E: write upload-q2-demo.mp4.command (or .sh/.bat per OS) containing `cdn upload "videos" "/tmp/test-fixtures/videos/q2-demo.mp4"` plus the curl HEAD self-verification, chmod +x, present_files, wait for 'done', then cdn_get_stats. Should NOT mention cdn_signed_upload_url or cdn_finalize_upload — the skill no longer invokes the MCP upload tools.

evals.name: mixed-batch
evals.prompt: I have a folder /tmp/test-fixtures/mixed/ with: three 50 KB thumbnail PNGs, one 4 MB hero JPG (I want this one preserved as-is, no compression), and one 120 MB MP4. All going to project 'launch'. What's the plan? Don't actually call the tools — just lay it out.
evals.expected_output: One path for everything: a single Path E script. Either one `cdn upload-dir "launch" "/tmp/test-fixtures/mixed"` line, or back-to-back `cdn upload` lines for the discrete files. No size-based splitting, no MCP base64 for the thumbnails, no compression (preserve is the default — compression is opt-in only). Verify via cdn_get_stats after the user reports done.
